title: anthoxanthum nitens subsp. balticum
intro: Anthoxanthum nitens subsp. balticum, also known as Baltic oat-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Anthoxanthum nitens subsp. balticum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile ingredient in traditional medicine.
